name: Butterscotch Walnut Bars
recipeInstructions:
CRUST: In a bowl, mix flour, salt, and sugar. Using a pastry blender, cut in butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Spread the mixture evenly into a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan and press firmly to create a compact layer. Bake at preheated 375°F oven for about 12 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove the pan from the rack while preparing the topping.
TOPPING: Combine syrup, butterscotch pieces, salt, butter, and water in a large heavy saucepan. Using a rubber spatula, stir over low heat, pressing butterscotch until melted and smooth. A few tiny unmelted pieces of butterscotch are acceptable. Quickly stir in walnuts and remove from heat. Working quickly to prevent the mixture from hardening, place spoonfuls onto the warm baked crust. Using the back of a spoon, spread the mixture within 1/2 to 3/4 inch of the crust edges (during baking, the topping will run toward the edges). Bake at 375°F oven for 10 minutes. Cool in the pan for 30 to 40 minutes or until only slightly warm.
With a small sharp knife, loosen around the edges, then cut into 16 bars. The bars in the center of the pan may appear too soft, but they will become firm as they cool; wait to remove them. Using a wide metal spatula, transfer the bars onto a cutting board and allow them to cool thoroughly until firm. Cut each bar in half.
